content:
# Station Reach Envelope Analysis

**Tool ID:** T090-01
**Block:** B090 (Station Design Optimisation)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Template
**Role:** Head Barista
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes per station
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. The best barista station is one where everything you need is within arm's reach and everything you do not need is somewhere else.

A ready-to-use template that removes the guesswork. Fill it in, adapt it to your reality, and put it to work.

**Built for Head Barista working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Template for mapping the reach envelope at each station, listing all items used during service and categorising them as within reach (keep), outside reach but frequently used (move closer), or rarely used (remove from station). This drives station redesign for speed and ergonomics.

This tool supports **Station Design Optimisation** (B090) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Head Barista
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes per station (do one station at a time)
**Frequency:** Annually. Also after any equipment change or when persistent bottlenecks are reported.

**What You Will Need:**
- This template (printed)
- Tape measure (or arm's length as approximate guide)
- Access to the station during a non-service period
- List of items currently at and around the station

**Steps:**
1. Stand at the station in your normal working position
2. Extend both arms -- everything within that arc is your "reach envelope" (approximately 60 cm radius)
3. List EVERY item at or near the station
4. Categorise each item using the frequency and reach criteria below
5. Mark items to KEEP, MOVE CLOSER, or REMOVE
6. Create an action plan for repositioning
7. Test the new layout during one shift before making it permanent

---

## Template

### Station Information

| Field | Details |
|-------|---------|
| Station name | Espresso Bar / POS / Food Prep / Wash-up / Other: _____ |
| Date | |
| Assessed by | |
| Station dimensions | _____ cm wide x _____ cm deep |
| Working position | Standing / Seated |
| Primary user role | |

### Reach Zones Definition

| Zone | Distance from Working Position | Rule |
|------|-------------------------------|------|
| **Zone A (Prime)** | Within arm's reach without moving feet (0-60 cm) | Items used 10+ times per hour MUST be here |
| **Zone B (Secondary)** | One step or a lean (60-120 cm) | Items used 3-10 times per hour can be here |
| **Zone C (Remote)** | Two+ steps required (120+ cm) | Items used fewer than 3 times per hour can be here |

### Espresso Bar Station Audit

| # | Item | Current Zone | Times Used Per Hour (estimate) | Correct Zone? | Action |
|---|------|-------------|-------------------------------|---------------|--------|
| 1 | Portafilter(s) | | 30+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 2 | Tamper | | 30+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 3 | Distribution tool | | 30+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 4 | Scales | | 20+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 5 | Timer (or machine timer) | | 20+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 6 | Knock box | | 20+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 7 | Grinder | | 20+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 8 | Milk jugs (clean, ready) | | 15+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 9 | Milk (whole) | | 15+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 10 | Milk (oat / alternative) | | 10+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 11 | Espresso cups | | 10+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 12 | Takeaway cups (most popular size) | | 10+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 13 | Lids (most popular size) | | 10+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 14 | Cleaning cloth (damp) | | 10+ | A | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 15 | Syrups (vanilla, caramel) | | 5-10 | A-B | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 16 | Chocolate powder / cocoa | | 3-5 | B | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 17 | Takeaway cups (other sizes) | | 3-5 | B | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 18 | Lids (other sizes) | | 3-5 | B | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 19 | Cup sleeves | | 3-5 | B | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 20 | Spare milk (backup fridge) | | 1-3 | B-C | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 21 | Other syrups (seasonal, less common) | | 1-3 | B-C | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 22 | Cleaning supplies (sanitiser, fresh cloths) | | 1-3 | B-C | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 23 | Drip tray cleaning brush | | 1-2 | C | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 24 | Machine cleaning powder (backflush) | | 1/day | C | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 25 | _____ | | | | Keep / Move / Remove |
| 26 | _____ | | | | Keep / Move / Remove |

### Items to Remove From Station Entirely

*These items are at the station but are NOT used during service. They create clutter and reduce workspace.*

| Item | Why It Is Here | Where It Should Go |
|------|---------------|-------------------|
| | | |
| | | |
| | | |

### Improvement Action Plan

| Priority | Item to Move | From (current) | To (proposed) | Effort (L/M/H) | Impact (L/M/H) | Owner | Done? |
|----------|-------------|----------------|---------------|-----------------|-----------------|-------|-------|
| 1 | | | | | | | |
| 2 | | | | | | | |
| 3 | | | | | | | |
| 4 | | | | | | | |
| 5 | | | | | | | |

### Before / After Comparison (after changes made)

| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Items in Zone A | | | |
| Items that should be in A but are not | | | |
| Items at station that are never used | | | |
| Estimated unnecessary steps per hour | | | |

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Signature | Date |
|------|------|-----------|------|
| Head Barista | | | |
| Manager (approval for changes) | | | |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Cluttering Zone A With Everything

**Why it happens:** Head Barista wants everything within reach, so they cram 25 items into 60 cm of counter space. The station becomes crowded and messy. Items get knocked over.
**How to prevent:** Zone A has limited space. Only items used 10+ times per hour belong there. Be ruthless. If it is used 5 times per hour, it lives in Zone B.
**If it is already happening:** Remove everything from the station. Put back ONLY the top 10 most-used items. See how much cleaner the workspace feels.

### Risk 2: Analysis Done on Paper But Never Implemented

**Why it happens:** The reach audit is completed, improvements identified, but nobody moves anything because it "works well enough."
**How to prevent:** Commit to implementing the top 3 changes within 1 week. Test during a single shift. Revert if it does not work.
**If it is already happening:** Pick the single highest-impact change (usually: move milk closer to the machine). Do it now. Assess after one rush.

### Risk 3: Only Auditing the Espresso Bar

**Why it happens:** The espresso bar is the most important station, so it gets all the attention. POS, food prep, and wash-up stations are ignored despite their own inefficiencies.
**How to prevent:** Complete this template for EVERY station, one per month. Use blank rows 25-26 to add station-specific items.
**If it is already happening:** Schedule the POS station audit for next month and food prep for the month after.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add filter/pour-over station as a separate audit. Include kettles, scales, timers, filter papers, decanters.
**Chain / Franchise:** Compare station layouts across locations. Standardise Zone A items based on brand standards.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Everything is Zone A in a kiosk. Focus on vertical organisation (shelves, hooks) to maximise limited counter space.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add bakery station audit: oven mitts, trays, tongs, display labels, wrapping materials.

**Making it yours:**
- Take a photo of each station before and after optimisation
- Mount a small "Station Standard" photo showing the ideal setup
- Review Zone A items whenever a new product is added (e.g., new syrup flavour)
- Ask the team: "What do you reach for most and what is in the way?"

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**

- T090-03 -- Five-S Station Audit Checklist (L2, checklist)
- T090-04 -- Ergonomic Station Assessment (L2, template)
- T090-05 -- Station Efficiency Benchmarking Report (L3, dashboard)

**Related blocks:**
- B089

**Parent block:** B090 -- Station Design Optimisation
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
